mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals resilient household demand and near-term revenue growth for index constituents, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as RSAFS growth rises.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 513356 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce positive 20-day return spreads versus lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above 2.25% in an expansion raises the discount rate applied to future earnings and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 2.42 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS crossings above 2.25% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward observations.

mechanism_narrative: Very low unemployment combined with slowing payroll growth identifies late-cycle labor-market saturation, weaker incremental income growth, and margin pressure, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0% and 3-month PAYEMS growth decelerates versus the prior 3-month growth rate
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 3.6 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-UNRATE and decelerating-PAYEMS condition is not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction term has insignificant predictive power at p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A negative 3-month change in industrial production signals deteriorating output and inventory demand, reducing expected earnings for cyclical sectors and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 109.1818 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO 3-month contractions are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered observations, or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1200 thousand units signal weakening interest-sensitive residential investment and softer demand for housing-linked goods, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 1139 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST readings below 1200 are not associated with lower subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns than readings above 1200 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth that exceeds consumer price growth identifies rising real consumption rather than inflation-only spending gains, supporting aggregate earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 254.958 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive real retail-sales growth does not outperform negative real retail-sales growth on subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the RSAFS-CPIAUCSL interaction coefficient is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising real GDP trend signals broad expansion in final demand and corporate revenue capacity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as GDPC1 growth increases.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value was 18912.326 as of four months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if higher quarterly GDPC1 growth fails to predict higher 60-day S&P 500 returns in expanding-window tests, or if return spreads between top and bottom GDPC1-growth terciles are nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate while the federal funds rate remains above 2.0% signals policy is restrictive as labor conditions weaken, raising recession risk and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is above 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 2.42 and UNRATE latest snapshot value was 3.6
failure_condition: Reject if the joint rising-UNRATE and high-FEDFUNDS condition is followed by nonnegative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction has no negative out-of-sample effect.
